trigger: User wants to create a new AI node on a single hive with no topology changes needed.
constraints: runtime field in node must exactly match the runtime name (no @hive suffix)
runtime version must be a semver string like '0.1.0'
ownership must be 'solution' for user-created nodes
do_not_repeat: do not add topology section if no new hive or VPN is needed
do not duplicate the runtime in nodes — nodes reference runtimes by name
do not set package_source to 'pre_published' unless the runtime already exists and is materialized

trigger: User wants a node on a worker hive and a routing rule so messages flow from motherbee to that worker.
constraints: always include the VPN entry when a new worker hive is added
route prefix must end with a dot (e.g., 'AI.specialist.' not 'AI.specialist')
next_hop_hive in routing must match the hive_id of the target hive exactly
do_not_repeat: do not add topology.hives for motherbee — it always exists
do not add a VPN for hives that already have one — check snapshot first
do not set next_hop_hive to a hive where the node does not live

trigger: User wants to deploy a workflow node with a workflow definition on an existing hive.
constraints: WF nodes must use a WF.* or wf.* runtime
wf_deployments.workflow_name must be unique per hive
wf_deployments.definition must be a non-empty object with at least a 'steps' array
do_not_repeat: do not put the workflow definition content inside the node config — it goes in wf_deployments
do not use 'WF.engine' as a node name — it is a runtime base, not a node
do not omit the runtime when WF node needs a custom package
